# UC-12.7.11 · Control-M Agent Availability and Heartbeat Gap Detection

## Description

Silent agent loss leaves jobs unrunnable on remote hosts while the schedule still assumes capacity exists. Heartbeat gaps are the fastest signal before a mountain of late jobs appears in the Enterprise Manager.

## Value

Reduces batch outage duration on distributed platforms and proves agent resiliency for audit questions.

## Implementation

Normalize timestamps to UTC; agents behind NAT may batch heartbeats—tune gap_sec per region. Enrich with CMDB owner and escalation tier. Pair with network device logs for path failures.

## SPL

```spl
index=batch sourcetype="controlm:agent"
| eval host=coalesce(agent_host, node_id, host)
| stats latest(_time) as last_seen, latest(status) as agent_status, values(message) as msgs by host
| eval gap_sec=now()-last_seen
| where gap_sec > 900 OR match(lower(agent_status),"(?i)down|disconnected|unavailable")
| sort -gap_sec
```

## Visualization

Choropleth or location map of agents, single-value (agents in gap), timeline of disconnect reasons.

- [BMC Control-M — Agent](https://documents.bmc.com/supportu/controlm/9.0.21/en-US/Documentation/Agent.htm)
